signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

signInInformation.asteriskText: *EuroLeagueTV is available worldwide excluding Spain, France, Italy and Monaco.

accountDeletionInfo.text:
Before continuing to delete your EuroLeague ID account, please bear in mind the following:

If you play the Euroleague Fantasy Challenge, you may want to delete your team there first, as it will be more difficult to do that if your account no longer exists. You can do that at [euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account](http://euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account).

club.info: Founded in 1936, Milan immediately took four consecutive domestic titles and – barring a few brief dry periods – the club has remained a regular winner of silverware ever since. The arrival of rising American star Bill Bradley helped turn Milan into a continental power as the club conquered the 1966 EuroLeague crown in front of its home fans. Milan was the EuroLeague runner-up the following year and then added three Saporta Cups to its trophy case between 1971 and 1976. The team captured the Korac Cup in 1985 and 1993 and consecutive EuroLeague championships in 1987 and 1988 as stars such as Mike D'Antoni, Dino Meneghin and Bob McAdoo led the way. A rough patch in the club's history in the late ‘90s was ended when fashion mogul Giorgio Armani became the team’s main sponsor early in the new millennium. Milan fought its way back into the EuroLeague and the club had a couple of Top 16 appearances before finally making the playoffs in 2014. That year also saw Milan win its first Italian League crown for nearly 20 years. Four-time EuroLeague champion head coach Ettore Messina was appointed in 2019 and helped Milan return to the Final Four for the first time in nearly three decades in 2021. Milan was eliminated in the 2022 playoffs and has not made the postseason since, but won three consecutive Italian Leagues from 2022 to 2024. Milan will be back with high hopes in the 2025-26 season.

heroMedia.title: Josh Nebo slays former club as Milan beats battling Maccabi
heroMedia.summary: The big man posted a double-double to boost the Italian side’s postseason hopes

linkedIds.text: EA7 Emporio Armani Milan v Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (2026-03-13T19:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
EA7 Emporio Armani Milan engineered a lifeline 96-87 home win over Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv to stay in the hunt for a play-in berth, while denting the Israeli side’s hopes of squeezing into the business end of Europe’s premier club competition.

The Italian team improved to 16-15 after grinding out its fifth win in the last seven games as Maccabi dropped to 14-16 after suffering a first defeat in five outings.

Center Josh Nebo put his former club Maccabi to the sword with a monster performance of 19 points and 10 rebounds, and Shavon Shields netted 11 of his 17 points in the fourth quarter as Milan repelled a fierce Maccabi fightback.

The visitors had slashed a 15-point third quarter deficit to just 84-80 in the home stretch before Shields sank a pair of successive three-pointers to put the game beyond Maccabi’s reach. Marko Guduric racked up 11 points and 8 assists for the home team, Zach LeDay scored 15 and Quinn Ellis added 12 as Milan gave itself a fighting chance of sneaking into the top 10 at the end of the Regular Season.

Nebo, who was a handful for his opponents all night, acknowledged that keeping their nerve when the chips were down in the fourth quarter was crucial for the home players.  

“In the past we had some trouble in the fourth quarter and today was a big step forward in responding to their run and closing the game out,” Nebo said in his postgame comments. “It was a tough game and we’re trying to push for a spot in the playoffs, so I try to bring my best effort to every matchup. The race is very tight so we’re trying to take it one game at a time and if we keep fighting like we did tonight, we’ve got a good chance,” he added.

Maccabi nosed ahead 25-26 at the end of the first quarter as Tamir Blatt beat the buzzer with an audacious three-pointer from the halfway line, but Milan then gained the upper hand and led 53-42 at halftime. The host appeared to be in for some plain sailing when it romped 59-44 ahead early in the third, but Maccabi had other ideas and always threatened to force a late twist before Shields delivered for Milan from long range.

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Olimpia Milano (@olimpiamilano1936)

The home team’s head coach Pepe Poeta was able to breathe a huge sigh of relief on the final buzzer after seeing his men close out the game. “We had three very solid quarters and we handled the endgame in a proper way,” he said. “It’s impossible to take away everything from a team as talented on offense as Maccabi but we did a good job limiting them and with this spirit we will try to reach the postseason.”

Lonnie Walker paced the visitors on 16 points and Gur Lavi, who averaged just 3.1 points per game before Friday night, served up a big first half when he poured in 13 of his 15 points, eventually finishing the game on a perfect 7-of-7 from the floor. Roman Sorkin and Jimmy Clark scored 13 apiece for Maccabi but the visitors were ultimately undone by Milan’s sound free throw shooting, as the home team netted 27 of 30 from the foul line.

“We played against a good team and they shot the ball really well,” Maccabi head coach Oded Kattash said. “We did a better job in the second half and the most important thing is that we kept fighting. We have a week to recover and get the next one.”

articleMetadata.title: Josh Nebo slays former club as Milan beats battling Maccabi

heroMedia.title: Milan staves off late Barcelona rally to secure pivotal win
heroMedia.summary: The hosts held on despite a dramatic fourth-quarter collapse

linkedIds.text: EA7 Emporio Armani Milan v FC Barcelona (2026-03-05T19:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
[EA7 Emporio Armani Milan](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/ea7-emporio-armani-milan/mil/)'s 87-84 victory over [FC Barcelona](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/fc-barcelona/bar/) moves the Italian team's record to 15-15 and snaps a two-game losing streak as the battle for playoff basketball rolls on. However, it was much harder work than it should have been down the stretch.

Milan set the pace early, racing out to a near-immediate lead and finishing the opening period seven points in front (24-17). Barcelona showed some resistance at the start of the second quarter, but it struggled to cope with Milan's increasingly fluid offense. By the time the teams headed into halftime, the hosts had extended their advantage to 12 points (46-34).

The game slipped further away from the visitors in the third quarter, when Peppe Poeta's side seized full control, dominating on both ends of the floor and stretching the margin to 27 (78-51) heading into the final period. But that's when things started to get interesting.

Barcelona produced an impressive offensive burst late on to cut the deficit to just two points (86-84) in the final seconds through deadly shot-making from [Tomas Satoransky](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/tomas-satoransky/002939/), but the comeback came far too late, and they ultimately fell short by three at the final buzzer.

content.title: EA7 Emporio Armani Milan-FC Barcelona | Round 30 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
"We have to take the positives of this game, forget about the last quarter and keep going," said Milan's [Leandro Bolmaro](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/leandro-bolmaro/008985/) after his team narrowly escaped the dramatic collapse.

Though the Milanese just about managed to hold onto the head-to-head tiebreaker after losing by two in Barcelona earlier in the season, the fans will certainly come away from the game with mixed emotions following a brutally poor final quarter that invited Barca back into the contest. After the game, Poeta remained encouraged by the majority of his team's performance.

"Maybe the best three quarters of the season," said Poeta. "In the fourth quarter, we just stopped playing. We struggled, we missed some wide-open shots, and it was a scary moment. But we managed to win, which is the most important thing. We also managed to get the tiebreaker, which is important. We showed a lack of patience. We can do better, but it's easier to work after a W."

Milan forward [Zach LeDay](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/zach-leday/008987/) played the starring role, scoring 16 points and grabbing 7 rebounds. Despite his major offensive contribution, he was more tuned into the team's defensive display.

"The best play for me was every time we played good defense, got a stop, and ran," said LeDay. "We just played defense, played together, and we will grow together. We have a lot to grow from, but I'm glad we won."

Play of the Year? pic.twitter.com/k7yl7fts1I

— Olimpia Milano (@OlimpiaMI1936) March 5, 2026

Bolmaro also made his mark on the game, recording 13 points, 4 rebounds and 6 assists. [Quinn Ellis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/quinn-ellis/012593/)'s defensive effort paid off as he combined 3 steals with his 5 assists. Bolstering the offense further, [Josh Nebo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/josh-nebo/011199/) (12 points) [Armoni Brooks](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/armoni-brooks/013384/) (11 points) and [Devin Booker](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/devin-booker/007056/) (10 points) also finished in double figures.

The home team managed to secure the win despite turning the ball over 19 times. Ultimately, Milan will be relieved to level its record and certainly won't underestimate the importance of the season's final stages.

"It was an important game for us – it was like a final," Bolmaro said. "The last eight or nine games will feel like a final for us. We need to get into the playoffs; that is our goal."

The next test for Milan comes against Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv next Friday, while Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (now 17-13) will aim to end their freefall when they welcome the hosts on the same night.

linkedId.text: EA7 Emporio Armani Milan v FC Barcelona (2026-03-05T19:30:00.000Z)

heroMedia.title: Diego Flaccadori sidelined with finger fracture
heroMedia.summary: Surgery was performed on his left hand injury

content.text:
[EA7 Emporio Armani Milan](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/ea7-emporio-armani-milan/mil/) has confirmed that guard [Diego Flaccadori](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/diego-flaccadori/005670/) will be reevaluated in six weeks' time after fracturing a finger on his left hand.

The player suffered a fractured fifth finger on his left hand, which was surgically corrected. Flaccadori will be reevaluated in six weeks, Milan confirmed.

🇮🇹 Diego Flaccadori ha riportato la frattura del quinto dito della mano sinistra che è stata corretta chirurgicamente. Il giocatore verrà rivalutato in 6 settimane

🇬🇧 Diego Flaccadori suffered a fractured fifth finger on his left hand, which was surgically corrected. The player…

— Olimpia Milano (@OlimpiaMI1936) March 5, 2026
Flaccadori, in his fifth EuroLeague season, has made seven appearances for Milan this season, averaging 2.0 points per game in limited minutes (5:54 per game).

articleMetadata.title: Diego Flaccadori sidelined with finger fracture
articleMetadata.description: EA7 Emporio Armani Milan has confirmed that guard Diego Flaccadori will be reevaluated in six weeks' time after fracturing a finger on his left hand.

heroMedia.title: Bryant Dunston to be reevaluated in six weeks after finger fracture
heroMedia.summary: The veteran big man required surgery for a compound fracture

content.text:
[EA7 Emporio Armani Milan](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/ea7-emporio-armani-milan/mil/) has confirmed that center [Bryant Dunston](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/bryant-dunston/003048/) will be reevaluated in six weeks' time after fracturing a finger on his left hand.

The 39-year-old, now in his 13th season in the EuroLeague and first with Milan, suffered a compound fracture to his second finger on his left hand that required surgery. The player will be reevaluated in six weeks, Milan confirmed on X.

🇮🇹Bryant Dunston ha riportato una frattura scomposta del secondo dito della mano sinistra per cui si è reso necessario un intervento chirurgico. Il giocatore verrà rivalutato in 6 settimane.

🇬🇧 Bryant Dunston suffered a compound fracture to his second finger on his left hand,…

— Olimpia Milano (@OlimpiaMI1936) March 5, 2026

Dunston, who has posted 3.1 points and 2.2 rebounds in 16 EuroLeague games for Milan this season, is a two-time champion after winning back-to-back titles with Anadolu Efes Istanbul in 2021 and 2022. He also secured back-to-back EuroLeague Best Defender awards in 2013-14 and 2014-15. His only All-EuroLeague selection came in 2016-17.

Heading into Round 30 of the 2025-26 season, Dunston ranks second all-time in blocks (375), seventh all-time in rebounds (1,630), sixth all-time in offensive rebounds (687), ninth all-time in two-pointers made (1,070), 15th all-time in PIR (4,029), ninth in games played (380), and joint 18th all-time in steals (280).

articleMetadata.title: Bryant Dunston to be reevaluated in six weeks after finger fracture
articleMetadata.description: EA7 Emporio Armani Milan has confirmed that center Bryant Dunston will be reevaluated in six weeks' time after fracturing a finger on his left hand.

heroMedia.title: Elijah Bryant powers Hapoel past Milan with statement performance
heroMedia.summary: The experienced guard led the charge, finishing with 25 points, 8 assists and 5 rebounds

linkedIds.text: Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv v EA7 Emporio Armani Milan (2026-02-26T19:05:00.000Z)

content.text:
[Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/hapoel-ibi-tel-aviv/hta/) burst onto the EuroLeague scene this season, with [Elijah Bryant](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/elijah-bryant/009846/) consistently delivering MVP-level performances. However, a four-game losing streak followed an injury that sidelined the Hapoel guard. Bryant returned just before the cup break and scored 17 points on the road at Zalgiris Kaunas, but his team fell again, extending the skid to five games.

This time, at home, things were different as Hapoel downed [EA7 Emporio Armani Milan](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/ea7-emporio-armani-milan/mil/) 92-86 to get back on track.

The 30-year-old got off to a strong start, scoring Hapoel’s first five points as the hosts carried a 29-19 lead into the second quarter. Milan responded and cut the deficit to 49-47 by halftime, shifting the momentum heading into the second half.

Hapoel’s defense stepped up in the third quarter, holding Milan to just 17 points. In the fourth, Bryant delivered again, scoring 9 of his team’s 19 points, including a dagger from deep that made it 88-77 with 2:18 remaining.

content.title: Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv-EA7 Emporio Armani Milan | Round 29 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Bryant finished with 25 points on 9-for-14 shooting. He also dished out 8 assists and grabbed 5 rebounds, making his presence felt on both ends of the floor. More importantly, when Milan ramped up the pressure late, he answered with tough shot-making to break down their defense.

“First off, it was the hustle, the defense, the rebounding – that was the clutch play,” [Collin Malcolm](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/collin-malcolm/012609/) said after the game when asked about the key to the win.

He then praised Bryant for stepping up when it mattered most.

“Obviously, Elijah hit some clutch shots to keep us ahead, and then the team did the job on the defensive end,” he added.

Bryant received key support from [Vasilije Micic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/vasilije-micic/002580/), who scored 18 points and tied his career high with 9 rebounds, as well as Swiss Army knife Malcolm, who filled the stat sheet with 10 points, 5 boards, 3 assists and 2 steals.

“I think it was obvious that we were the better team on the court against a very good opponent,” Hapoel head coach Dimitris Itoudis told EuroLeague TV. “I think we played good basketball today. We played great basketball on both ends.”

הפועל מנצחת ♨️ pic.twitter.com/mLXk2Mll3N

— Hapoel Tel Aviv BC (@HapoelTLVBC) February 26, 2026

With the five-game losing streak snapped, the Israeli club now looks to build momentum heading into a demanding stretch run. Hapoel has both the talent and the depth to compete for a high finish in its first EuroLeague campaign. The missing piece remains consistency – the trait that separates teams down the stretch.

“We need to be more consistent, but as I said, we are a new team trying to find our chemistry,” Itoudis said in the postgame press conference. “It’s not easy, but we’re working on it. Chemistry-wise, we need more games, more practice, and more trust in each other. This team has shown it has quality, and we need to build on it.”

Next week could be pivotal for Hapoel’s season. The team hosts Paris Basketball, [which stunned Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens on the road](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/pulsating-paris-poop-panathinaikoss-party-as-hayes-davis-debut-falls-short/) on Thursday night, before facing Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv in a highly anticipated Israeli derby.

With Bryant back in rhythm, Hapoel will aim to start a winning streak that could push it into the top six. From there, game by game and practice by practice, the consistency Itoudis has been calling for may follow.

linkedId.text: Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv v EA7 Emporio Armani Milan (2026-02-26T19:05:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Elijah Bryant powers Hapoel past Milan with statement performance
articleMetadata.description: Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv burst onto the EuroLeague scene this season, with Elijah Bryant consistently delivering MVP-level performances. However, a five-game losing skid threatened to derail the team's season, but on Thursday night Hapoel downed EA7 Emporio Armani Milan 92-86 to get back on track.

heroMedia.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

content.text:
The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

When Phase 2 opened on February 26 at 10:00 CET, fans once again responded in massive numbers. Following Phase 1, which saw more than one million fans connect to the ticketing platform and resulted in a complete sellout, the second allocation was also quickly exhausted, reaffirming the immense appeal of European basketball’s premier event.

The overwhelming global interest highlights the unique atmosphere and significance of the Final Four, with fans from across Europe and beyond eager to secure their place in Athens for the season’s ultimate showdown.

Fans who were unable to purchase tickets during General public tickets sale Phase 2 will still have an additional opportunity on February 26 as VIP tickets will be available starting at 14:00 CET.

Fans who were unable to secure tickets during Phase 1 will still have multiple opportunities to attend European basketball’s premier event. The second public ticket sales phase will open on February 26 at 10:00 CET. The third public ticket sales phase will open on March 17 at 10:00 CET, while the final sales phase will begin on April 20 at 10:00 CEST, coinciding with the conclusion of the Regular Season as teams continue their pursuit of a Final Four berth.

Each remaining phase will feature a limited allocation across multiple price categories, and high demand is expected once again.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

articleMetadata.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

heroMedia.title: Power rankings: After the cups have gone
heroMedia.summary: Our experts offer up a suggestion for every team through 28 rounds

content.text:
Welcome to the latest edition of the EuroLeague Power Rankings. The list is the brainchild of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net)'s team of experts, which is made up of Geoff Gillingham, Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Igor Petrinovic. The rankings are not a prediction of what the final standings will look like, but rather where all 20 teams stand – in their humble opinions – today, after Round 28.

 

### 1. Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (20-7)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Besiktas on Sunday to win the Turkish Cup

**The question we are asking:** Is Fenerbahce aware that not team to finish the regular season in first place under the current format has won the championship?

 

### 2. Olympiacos Piraeus (18-9)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Panathinaikos in the Greek Cup final on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Do we need to start worrying about the health of the Reds’ stars?

 

### 3. Real Madrid (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Baskonia in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can *Los Blancos* improve its defense to become a top contender?

### 4. Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Olympiacos to win the Greek Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Will the addition of Nigel Hayes-Davis be enough to put the Greens over the top?

 

### 5. Valencia Basket (18-10)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** How will Valencia shake its disappointment from not winning the Spanish Cup at home? 

 

### 6. FC Barcelona (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Barcelona in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** With Punter back in action, can Barça improve its consistency and stay in the top-six race?

 

### 7. Zalgiris Kaunas (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Rytas Vilnius in the Lithuanian Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** With six home games remaining and good momentum, is a top-six finish a realistic goal?

### 8. AS Monaco (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Le Mans to win the French Leaders Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After a turbulent stretch on and off the court, can this trophy galvanize Monaco and stabilize its EuroLeague push?

 

### 9. Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (16-11)

**What they did last week:** Went 3-0 in the Israeli League

**The question we are asking:** Is Hapoel’s recent slide just a bump in the road or a sign that the team peaked too early?

 

### 10. Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Mega Belgrade to win the Serbian Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Jordan Nwora the star who can carry Zvezda to new heights? 

 

### 11. Dubai Basketball (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bosna Sarajevo in the ABA League

**The question we are asking:** Now that Dzanan Musa is back and in shape, can Dubai win enough games to make it to the Play-In Showdown?

### 12. Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (13-15)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bnei Herzliya to win the Israeli Cup on Thursday

**The question we are asking:** Is there enough time left in the season for Maccabi to climb to 10th place?

 

### 13. EA7 Emporio Armani Milan (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Tortona to win the Italian Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can Milan translate domestic momentum into EuroLeague consistency and revive its play-in hopes?

 

### 14. FC Bayern Munich (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bamberg in German Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What would this team have looked like with a full season under Coach Pesic?

 

### 15. Anadolu Efes Istanbul (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Besiktas in the Turkish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Efes destined to become a spoiler for a few teams in the playoff race?

### 16. Virtus Bologna (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Tortona in the Italian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What adjustments can Dusko Ivanovic make to halt Virtus’s slide – especially away from home?

 

### 17. Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Mega Belgrade in the Serbian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Can Joan Penarroya realign the locker room and restore Partizan’s competitive edge?

 

### 18. Paris Basketball (9-18)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bourg in the French Leader’s Cup quarterfinals on Friday

**The question we are asking:** What will it take for Paris to recapture its early-season identity and get Nadir Hifi back to his best?

 

### 19. Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After its Spanish Cup success and strong home performances, can Baskonia start winning consistently on the road?

 

### 20. LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (7-21)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Monaco in the French Cup quarterfinals on Tuesday

**The question we are asking:** With the postseason out of reach, which players are auditioning for their EuroLeague futures?

heroMedia.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
heroMedia.summary: Phase 1 general public ticket sale attracted over 1 million fans worldwide

content.text:
Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

The launch of Phase 2 coincides with the closing of the EuroLeague players’ market, ensuring that fans will have a clear picture of each team’s final roster.

All fans,  except those who have already purchased tickets during previous sales phases or pre-sales , will be eligible to access the February 26 general public sale starting at 10:00 CET. During this second sales window,  all fans will also have the opportunity to purchase VIP tickets starting at 14:00 CET .

Ticketing information is available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/). Fans must log in or create a EuroLeague ID account to complete their purchase. Once logged in, all available categories will be displayed. Due to expected high demand and to ensure fairness, each fan may purchase a maximum of two tickets.

For the first time, Final Four tickets are being released in multiple sales phases. This approach is designed to expand access for the EuroLeague’s growing global fan base, offering multiple opportunities to secure tickets,  whether fans plan to attend regardless of the qualified teams or prefer to wait until the competition reaches its decisive stages. All phases follow a fair access policy, meaning that fans who purchase tickets in one phase will not be eligible to participate in subsequent phases. This measure is designed to ensure broader access to the event.

Phase 1, held on February 11, experienced massive demand, as over a million fans connected to sold out all available tickets in less than five minutes, once again demonstrating the enormous appeal of the EuroLeague’s premier event.

The 2026 EuroLeague Final Four semifinals will be played on Friday, May 22, at 18:00 and 21:00 local time (17:00 and 20:00 CEST). On Sunday, May 24, the adidas NextGen EuroLeague Championship Game will tip off at 18:00 local time (17:00 CEST), while the season will culminate with the Championship Game at 21:00 local time (20:00 CEST), when a new EuroLeague champion will be crowned.

A third ticket sales window will open on Tuesday, March 17, at 10:00 CET, offering another opportunity for fans to secure their seats. The final sales phase will launch on Monday, April 20, at 10:00 CEST, positioned between the conclusion of the Regular Season and the start of the Play-In Showdown, with 10 teams still in contention for a place in the Final Four.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

articleMetadata.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
articleMetadata.description: Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

heroMedia.title: Milan captures the Italian Cup for the ninth time

content.text:
EA7 Emporio Armani Milan captured the Italian Cup for the first time since 2022 by coming from behind to defeat Bertram Derthona Tortona 85-77 in the final on Sunday. This marked Milan's ninth Italian Cup, which broke a tie with Virtus Bologna and Treviso for the most Italian Cup triumphs in history.  

Armoni Brooks paced the Milan attack with 20 points on 6-of-10 three-point shooting. Marko Guduric scored half of his 18 points in the fourth quarter to spark Milan when it was trailing. Josh Nebo posted 14 points and 11 rebounds, Zach LeDay added 12 points and Leandro Bolmaro had 11. Prentiss Hubb paced Tortona with 23 points and Justin Gorham contributed 13. 

LeDay scored twice as Milan raced out to a 10-3 start. Then Nebo highlighted an 11-0 run as the margin grew to 28-11 before settling at 31-16 after 10 minutes. Tortona turned the tables in the second quarter, with Hubb and Gorham leading a 0-14 charge to make it 42-40. Milan held a slim lead, 47-45, at halftime.  

The first points of the second half were a three-point play by Gorham that gave Tortona its first lead since the opening minutes. That’s how things stayed for the rest of the third quarter, with Tortona remaining ahead, but never by more than 6 points. The scoreboard showed 59-62 at the end of the quarter. Guduric heated up early in the fourth quarter as Milan tied the score and then went ahead. The lead swung back and forth for a few minutes before Bolmaro and Brooks pushed the difference to 77-70. Milan held on the rest of the way.

articleMetadata.title: Milan captures the Italian Cup for the ninth time
